ddls:
CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device type */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical or logical location of the device */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the device */,
  "os_version" TEXT /* Operating system version of the device */,
  "hardware_version" TEXT /* Hardware version of the device */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_devices_device_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_type_id") REFERENCES device_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the device type (e.g., Switch, Router) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the device type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_ports" (
  "port_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each port */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device */,
  "port_name" TEXT /* Name of the port */,
  "port_type" TEXT /* Type of the port (e.g., Ethernet, Gigabit) */,
  "port_status" TEXT /* Status of the port (e.g., up, down) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("port_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_ports_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "connections" (
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each connection */,
  "start_port_id" INTEGER /* ID of the starting port */,
  "end_port_id" INTEGER /* ID of the ending port */,
  "bandwidth" INTEGER /* Bandwidth allocated for the connection */,
  "vlan_id" INTEGER /* VLAN ID for the connection */,
  "vlan_mode" TEXT /* Mode of the VLAN (e.g., Access, Trunk) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("connection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_connections_end_port_id FOREIGN KEY ("end_port_id") REFERENCES device_ports ("port_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_connections_start_port_id FOREIGN KEY ("start_port_id") REFERENCES device_ports ("port_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_connections_vlan_id FOREIGN KEY ("vlan_id") REFERENCES vlans ("vlan_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vlans" (
  "vlan_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each VLAN */,
  "vlan_name" TEXT /* Name or description of the VLAN */,
  "vlan_type" TEXT /* Type of VLAN (e.g., Access, Trunk) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vlan_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vlan_memberships" (
  "membership_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each membership */,
  "port_id" INTEGER /* ID of the port */,
  "vlan_id" INTEGER /* ID of the VLAN */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("membership_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vlan_memberships_vlan_id FOREIGN KEY ("vlan_id") REFERENCES vlans ("vlan_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vlan_memberships_port_id FOREIGN KEY ("port_id") REFERENCES device_ports ("port_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* User's login name */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, technician) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken (e.g., created, modified) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Date and time of the action */,
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* ID of the connection affected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_connection_id FOREIGN KEY ("connection_id") REFERENCES connections ("connection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bandwidth_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each usage record */,
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* ID of the connection being monitored */,
  "usage_amount" INTEGER /* Amount of bandwidth used in Mbps */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Date and time of the recorded usage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bandwidth_usage_connection_id FOREIGN KEY ("connection_id") REFERENCES connections ("connection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alarms" (
  "alarm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alarm */,
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* ID of the connection that triggered the alarm */,
  "alarm_type" TEXT /* Type of alarm (e.g., high bandwidth usage, connection down) */,
  "alarm_status" TEXT /* Status of the alarm (e.g., active, cleared) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Date and time the alarm was triggered */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alarm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alarms_connection_id FOREIGN KEY ("connection_id") REFERENCES connections ("connection_id")
)
